steps.title: Find social pressure
steps.content: The claim says Newland follows social conventions dictated by other men in the audience. The supporting quotation must therefore show those men collectively shaping what Newland accepts or does, especially on questions of acceptable conduct.

steps.title: Reject unrelated quotations
steps.content: A describes the opera setting. B describes Newland's private surprise at life continuing as before. C says Newland feels superior to the men, not that they direct him. The remaining quotation says the grouped men represented "New York" and that masculine solidarity made him accept their moral doctrine. That directly illustrates social convention controlling him. The answer is D.
